summary: Soundboard capture of the June 28, 1969 show delivers solid audio (minor hiss/brightness quirks) while showcasing a country‑infused set highlighted by a stellar “Doin’ That Rag” and “High Time.”
review: The recording is a soundboard source that sounds clear enough for casual listening, with only occasional background hiss or a slight treble imbalance that can be EQ’d out. Musically the band is in a transitional phase, trading psychedelic jams for folk‑rock and country flavors; Jerry’s pedal‑steel intro on “Doin’ That Rag,” his vocal on “Me & My Uncle,” and Pigpen’s bluesy contributions are repeatedly praised. Overall the performance feels tight, energetic, and worth revisiting.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 5) and enthusiastic comments about both the music and recording indicate a strong show with good‑to‑excellent audio quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and listenable; occasional background hiss on OGG, slight brightness/dullness variations likely from Dolby B encoding, but no major drop‑outs or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set reflects the band’s shift from late‑’68 psychedelic explorations to a folk/country/Working Man’s Dead direction, with tight transitions (e.g., pedal‑steel intro into “Doin' That Rag” leading into “High Time”).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for pedal‑steel intro on “Doin’ That Rag,” ripping licks on “Sittin’ On Top of the World,” strong vocal on “Me & My Uncle,” and overall energetic playing.
